mnemonics: ARMA - Always Remember Mean Adjustment (for AR and MA models to be stationary)
PACF Cuts Off for AR, ACF Cuts Off for MA (helps identify model type from plots)
Invertibility is the MA's Stationarity (MA models require invertibility condition, analogous to stationarity for AR)
keyPoints: For an AR(1) model, the autocorrelation function (ACF) decays exponentially. The estimator for the AR parameter φ is approximately normally distributed for large samples.
For an MA(1) model, the ACF has a single significant spike at lag 1. The estimation is more complex than for AR models and requires iterative methods.
The invertibility condition for an MA model (that its MA polynomial has roots outside the unit circle) is crucial for estimation and ensures the model can be represented as an infinite AR process.
commonMistakes: Confusing the identification properties of AR and MA models (e.g., thinking an ACF that cuts off after lag 1 indicates an AR(1) instead of an MA(1)).
Assuming the distribution of estimators is exactly normal in small samples; the normality is a large-sample approximation.
Forgetting to check the stationarity (for AR) or invertibility (for MA) conditions after estimating the model parameters.
